pdg_node.informal_desc: Entry-point pure transform for inverse Schmitt trigger behavior. No sub-methods, mutable attributes, or config-gated branches were provided, so this is modeled as a single stateless atom.

declaration.name: inverse_schmitt_trigger_transform
declaration.docstring: Entry-point pure transform for inverse Schmitt trigger behavior. No sub-methods, mutable attributes, or config-gated branches were provided, so this is modeled as a single stateless atom.
declaration.conceptual_summary: hysteretic_inverted_bistable_threshold_mapper. Transforms a numeric signal into a two-state output by applying dual-threshold switching with inverted polarity. State changes occur only when the signal crosses the outer bounds; values inside the intermediate band preserve the previously emitted state, creating hysteresis. Over ordered samples, this yields a piecewise-constant state trajectory that suppresses rapid oscillations caused by near-boundary noise.. Applications: Biomedical instrumentation: convert noisy continuous sensor waveforms into stable phase/event labels for downstream analysis., Robotics and embedded control: debounced mode switching from fluctuating proximity, force, or orientation measurements., Communication systems: robust symbol/state gating from analog channel metrics that fluctuate around decision boundaries., Infrastructure monitoring: persistent regime-change detection from vibration or strain amplitudes under high measurement noise.
